name: MoneyGram
description_short: MoneyGram operates a nationwide network of money transfer and check-cashing locations, enabling consumers to send/receive money and access cash services in person or online.
description_long:
MoneyGram is an established money transfer and financial services company that operates an extensive physical location network across the United States. The company specializes in facilitating both domestic and international money transfers, making it one of the primary competitors in the remittance and cash access market.

MoneyGram's core service offerings center on money transfers (send and receive), check cashing, money orders, wire transfers, and bill payment services. The company provides flexibility by allowing customers to conduct transactions either online through their digital platform or in person at one of their numerous retail locations. Their location-finder tool enables consumers to search by address, city, state, or ZIP code, emphasizing the convenience of their widespread physical footprint.

MoneyGram's primary distinguishing factor is their combination of digital and physical distribution channels. While many competitors have shifted primarily online, MoneyGram maintains an extensive brick-and-mortar network at retail partners, making their services accessible to unbanked and underbanked consumers who prefer in-person transactions. This hybrid model appeals to customers seeking both convenience and the security of face-to-face transactions for larger transfers.

MoneyGram serves a critical function in the financial services ecosystem for consumers without traditional banking relationships or those needing quick cash access and transfer services. However, customers should note that the website content provided is primarily a location-finder tool with limited detail on fee structures, transfer limits, or specific service terms, requiring consumers to contact locations directly or visit their full website for complete pricing and policy information.
pros: Nationwide network of physical locations accessible to consumers preferring in-person transactions
Dual-channel access: both online and in-person transaction options available
Easy-to-use location finder searchable by address, city, state, or ZIP code
Established brand with long operating history in money transfer and check-cashing sector
Serves unbanked and underbanked populations without requiring traditional bank accounts
Multiple service types consolidated at single locations (transfers, check cashing, money orders, bill pay)
Same-location send and receive capabilities for domestic money transfers
cons: Website lacks transparent fee schedule or pricing information for any services
No APR, interest rate, or cost comparison data available on the public-facing location page
Limited detail on transfer limits, processing times, or service restrictions
Physical location dependency for some transactions may be inconvenient for some users
No information about identity verification requirements or documentation needed
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers without traditional bank accounts
International remittance senders supporting family members abroad
Consumers preferring in-person financial transactions over digital-only platforms
Individuals needing quick access to cash through check cashing or money orders
diagnosis: MoneyGram is best for consumers without traditional banking relationships who need reliable access to check cashing, money transfers, and cash services through a nationwide physical network. The main caveat is that the available website content is primarily a location-finder tool with minimal information about fees, rates, or specific service terms, requiring direct contact with locations for detailed pricing and policy information.
services: International money transfers (receive money from abroad)
In-person transaction services at retail locations
Cash pickup services for received transfers

places_enrichment.adr_format_address: 2956 E 10th St, Jeffersonville, IN 47130, USA
